label: 410_xi_uschina relations_uschina_visit
label_human: US-China relations under Obama and hu
description: The state visits between Barack Obama and Hu Jintao highlighted the evolving dynamics of U.S.-China relations. Discussions centered on economic ties, political systems, and social movements like Occupy Hong Kong, reflecting broader tensions in bilateral interactions.

earliest_articles.title: Around the Halls: The State of the Union and Foreign Policy
earliest_articles.excerpt: President Obama’s first State of the Union address was a pivotal moment for Obama’s presidency, where he articulated policies and initiatives that will define his first term. Scholars from around the halls of Brookings offer their thoughts on what the speech means for U.S. foreign policy, and how it is likely to be…

earliest_articles.title: Frustrated United States Struggles to Open Dialogue with China
earliest_articles.excerpt: It is sometimes said Democrats know nothing about Asia and prefer to swan around with Europeans. Yet less than six months after his trip to North-East Asia (and just as the White House announces that he will miss a forthcoming European summit) Barack Obama is returning to our neighbourhood. newest_articles.title: Obama’s past and Biden’s future with China
newest_articles.excerpt: U.S.-China relations have deteriorated in recent years, and are expected to face a number of difficult challenges across a number of economic, geopolitical, and diplomatic platforms with the incoming Biden administration. Brookings Senior Fellow Jeffrey A. Bader speaks about his time as senior director for East Asian…

central_articles.title: Assessing U.S.-China relations under the Obama administration
central_articles.excerpt: In weighing the state of U.S.-China relations, I am reminded of a well-known story. When Zhou Enlai, who served as China’s premier from 1949 to 1976, was asked for his opinion of the 1789 French Revolution, he demurred: “It’s too early to say.” To analyze history as it unfolds presents an even riskier and more…
